name: Omelette with Zucchini Tomato and Basil
recipeIngredient: 12 eggs (2 to 3 per person), pinch of salt and pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. TO MAKE THE FILLING: In a medium skillet, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the shallot and sauté until soft, about 2 minutes. Add the mushrooms and continue to sauté another 2 minutes. Add the zucchini and continue to sauté for 2 minutes.
2. Add the tomato and cook over high heat for 2 minutes to evaporate any excess liquid. Add the salt, pepper, basil, and mix well. Taste for seasoning. Cover and keep warm.
3. TO PREPARE EACH OMELETTE: Whisk 2 to 3 eggs with a pinch of salt, a pinch of pepper, and ½ teaspoon club soda until smooth.
4. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in an 8-inch omelette pan or skillet over medium heat until it begins to sizzle.
5. Pour in the egg mixture and stir gently in the center of the pan with the flat side of a fork. Lift the edges of the omelette so any uncooked egg runs to the edge of the pan.
6. Vigorously slide the pan back and forth over the heat until the omelette begins to slip around freely.
7. When the omelette is lightly cooked but still creamy in the center, spoon about 2 tablespoons of the filling on the half closer to the handle. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon parmesan cheese on top.
8. Quickly jerk the pan toward you with the handle so that the other half flips over on top of the filling. Slide the folded omelette onto a serving dish and serve immediately or keep warm in a low oven while preparing the remaining omelettes.
